message:
fix(command,runtime): fix pcall/xpcall Vec performance, MULTI queuing order, appenddirname path, and CONFIG SET keyspace-events atomicity

lua_eval.rs — pcall and xpcall return value construction:
  Replace `vals.insert(0, LuaValue::Bool(true))` with a pre-allocated
  Vec that pushes the success boolean first, then extends with the
  return values. The previous `insert(0, ...)` on a Vec shifts every
  element right by one (O(n)), which is unnecessary when we can simply
  build the result in the correct order from the start. Both the pcall
  and xpcall success paths are updated identically.

lib.rs — MULTI/EXEC transaction command queuing moved earlier:
  The MULTI transaction queuing logic was previously positioned after
  the runtime-special command dispatch block. This meant that commands
  like CONFIG, ACL, CLIENT, SELECT, WAIT, SUBSCRIBE, and other
  runtime-special commands would execute immediately even inside a
  MULTI block instead of being queued for atomic EXEC execution. The
  queuing check is now performed before the runtime-special dispatch,
  with an explicit allowlist of commands that genuinely must execute
  immediately inside MULTI (MULTI, EXEC, DISCARD, WATCH, UNWATCH,
  QUIT, RESET). Subscribe/Unsubscribe family commands are now
  explicitly rejected inside MULTI with an appropriate error message,
  matching Redis behavior.

lib.rs — CONFIG GET appenddirname returns full parent path:
  Previously, `appenddirname` extracted only the final component of
  the AOF config path's parent directory via `.file_name()`. This is
  incorrect when the path has multiple components (e.g., for
  "data/appendonly/my.aof" it would return "appendonly" instead of
  "data/appendonly"). Changed to use `.to_string_lossy()` on the full
  parent path, with a `.filter(|p| !p.is_empty())` guard so that a
  bare filename like "custom.aof" (whose parent is "") falls through
  to the default "appendonlydir" — but now correctly returns "." for
  a relative single-component path via the new unit test.

lib.rs — CONFIG SET notify-keyspace-events deferred application:
  The keyspace events flag was being applied immediately during CONFIG
  SET parameter parsing, before all parameters in a multi-param CONFIG
  SET had been validated. If a later parameter failed validation, the
  keyspace events would already be changed, violating the atomicity
  guarantee of CONFIG SET (all-or-nothing). The flags are now stored
  in `next_keyspace_events` and applied only after all parameters pass
  validation, consistent with how other CONFIG SET parameters (hz,
  appendonly, slowlog-max-len, etc.) are handled.

lib.rs — new test config_get_appenddirname_uses_dot_for_filename_only_aof_path:
  Adds a regression test that verifies CONFIG GET appenddirname returns
  "." when the AOF path is a bare filename with no directory component,
  ensuring the empty-string-to-default fallback works correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
